title: The Coffee Talk Was Load-Bearing — what organizations quietly lose when the assistant answers first

options.reader: a warm, clear-eyed senior colleague speaking at eye level to an entire organization — from the boardroom to the team room
options.audience: everyone in an organization adopting AI — executives, managers, architects and individual contributors alike
options.slide_style: A keynote for everyone in an organization: warm, composed, minimal text — the slides give names to phenomena, so let coined terms carry slides (the unasked question, the who-knows-what map, co-located strangers, quiet outsourcing, the merge ritual, cognitive debt). Design figures that teach: the unasked-question flow (what silently does not happen when the assistant answers), the Stack Overflow question-volume cliff (peak ~200k/month 2014, ~108k Nov 2022, collapse >90% since — from the data in the source), the four losses as a map (socialization, weak ties, who-knows-what, apprenticeship), and the outsourcing mirror (codified interface vs shared context, then vs now). Use big_statement slides for the load-bearing lines: 'The coffee talk was load-bearing', 'The questions did not disappear — the commons did', 'Argument is the process', 'Being a good colleague is infrastructure'. End with a Sources slide (or two) listing every reference with its URL in small text, taken from the Sources section.
